authors_affiliation: David Assaraf, Jeremy Levy, Janmajay Singh, Armand Chocron, Joachim A. Behar — Faculty of Biomedical Engineering, Technion-IIT, Haifa, Israel (Janmajay Singh: independent researcher)

result.evaluation_split: Local 80%/20% stratified train-test split and 5-fold cross-validation on training data only; authors state they 'did not manage to score our models on the Challenge hidden test set' for technical reasons

self_reported_metrics.other: Challenge score (self-reported, not official): Feature-Engineering (FE) model 0.65 local test / 0.66+/-0.02 5-fold CV; Deep Learning (DL) model 0.52 local test / 0.54+/-0.04 5-fold CV; Ensemble (EL) model 0.66 local test / 0.66+/-0.03 5-fold CV. Per-class F-beta scores also reported in Table 3 (e.g. F_AF: FE 0.91, DL 0.41, EL 0.92; F_SNR: FE 0.80, DL 0.90, EL 0.86).

model.architecture_details: Combines two independently trained models via logistic-regression stacking: (1) a feature-engineering (FE) pipeline — 113 morphological biomarkers (via wavedet delineation) plus 16 HRV features per lead, plus age/sex, fed into one-vs-rest random forest classifiers (750 estimators, max depth 70) with mRMR + floating recursive feature addition selection; (2) a deep learning (DL) model — 8 CNN cells (Conv1d-BatchNorm-ReLU-Conv1d-BatchNorm-ReLU-MaxPool-Dropout) with shortcut/residual connections every 2 cells, followed by a GRU layer and a multi-head fully-connected output (binary SNR-vs-rest head plus a 23-class multi-head).
model.key_novelty: Directly compares a classical digital-biomarker feature-engineering approach (HRV + morphological features feeding a random forest) against a deep CNN-GRU representation-learning approach, then stacks both via a logistic-regression ensemble to combine their complementary strengths.

input.signal_length_window: Variable-length recordings; DL model inputs zero-padded to a fixed 40,000 samples (80 s at 500 Hz); FE approach used a representative ECG cycle per lead for waveform delineation
input.sampling_rate: Resampled to 500 Hz (native rates varied: 257/500/1000/2000 Hz across source databases)
input.preprocessing: Electrode-switch detection and polarity correction; signal-quality screening via bSQI index (examples with bSQI < 0.8 discarded, ~3213 excluded); Butterworth band-pass filter (0.13-88 Hz); two notch filters at 50 Hz and 60 Hz for power-line interference

data.training_datasets: All 6 official challenge training databases (CPSC, CPSC-2, Georgia, PTB, PTB-XL, INCART) plus two external open-access datasets: Chapman University/Shaoxing People's Hospital ("CUSPH", 10646 examples) and Chapman University/Ningbo First Hospital ("CUNFH", 334 examples, mostly PVC); total n=53730 examples after quality/label exclusions
data.class_imbalance_handling: Minority classes proportionally over-weighted in the one-vs-rest random forest classifiers; reduced problem to 24 multilabel classes by merging 3 pairs the challenge treats as equivalent (CRBBB/RBBB, PVC/VPB, PAC/SVPB)
data.augmentation_techniques: not reported (class imbalance addressed via external real-world databases and class re-weighting rather than synthetic augmentation)

training.loss_function: Random forest: not applicable (Gini-based splits); Deep learning: weighted combination of BCEWithLogitsLoss and F-beta score loss, with the loss weighting treated as a tunable hyperparameter
training.cross_validation_strategy: Stratified 5-fold cross-validation for hyperparameter tuning of both the FE (random forest) and DL models; final evaluation on an 80%/20% stratified train-test split

contribution.summary: Shows that engineered digital biomarkers (HRV + 113 morphological features per lead) fed into a random forest outperform an end-to-end CNN-GRU deep learning model on this task, and that stacking both in an ensemble yields the best overall local score.
